title: Use JSONB without losing the relational spine
description: Keep queried, constrained, and joined fields as columns; reserve JSONB for genuinely variable or sparse data.
body: Use when tempted to store a whole entity as one JSONB blob. Do not push fields you filter, join, or constrain on into JSONB just to avoid migrations. Actions: keep identifiers, foreign keys, and frequently-filtered fields as typed columns with constraints; use JSONB for sparse/variable attributes; index JSONB containment queries with a gin index (jsonb_path_ops for @> ) or expression indexes on extracted fields. Validation signals include EXPLAIN showing a gin/expression index used for the JSONB predicate instead of a full scan. Risk boundary: putting foreign keys or money fields inside JSONB forfeits constraints and type safety; flag it as a data-integrity risk.
